Add homepage to package.json

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: nuclear fix for system prompt parroting — stop loading CLAUDE.md/CKB

CLAUDE.md, SESSION_STATE.md, and JarvisxWill_CKB.md are development
documentation for Claude Code sessions, not for a Telegram chatbot.
Loading them gave the LLM thousands of quotable phrases it would parrot
in conversation ("built in a cave", "Minds converge", "not a DEX",
"cooperative capitalism", etc.).

No amount of regex sanitization could fully prevent fragments leaking.
The fix: don't load these files at all. Replace with a 2-sentence
project brief. The system prompt already contains everything the bot
needs for personality, communication rules, and behavior.

Also expanded output sanitizer with 15+ additional poison patterns
and added proactive engagement suppression for cross-bot addressing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v68.6.0: Performance & styling improvements (#104)

* Improve responsiveness, night mode UX, clothes grid, and load performance

- Fix scroll-to-top button: add missing position:fixed, opacity:0, transition,
  and pointer-events:none base state so it correctly hides/shows on scroll
- Fix bottom nav: switch hardcoded dark background to var(--card) so it adapts
  to both dark and light themes; add body.light .bottom-nav override
- Fix light mode: correct skeleton background-size (200%→800px), add chip.active
  override, panel/panel-sm overrides, and scroll-top-btn light style
- Add VirtualGrid gridClass prop; apply closet-grid class to wardrobe grid so
  CSS breakpoints actually control column counts
- Add responsive closet grid: 2 columns on ≤340px, 4 columns on ≥520px,
  compact photo height on ≤360px screens
- Add modulepreload hints for main.js, app.js, data.js, engine.js, utils.js
  to kick off module fetches before React CDN scripts finish parsing
- Add layout containment to bottom-nav-inner to reduce repaint scope
- Bump version to 68.6.0 to trigger service worker cache refresh

https://claude.ai/code/session_019pS9X1R8PVd74VCEqX1VYs

* Apply all audit recommendations: perf, a11y, UX, PWA hardening

Bug fixes:
- Fix useEffect safety-cleanup running every render (add dep array)
- Add type="button" to scroll-top button (prevent accidental form submit)

Performance:
- Cache weather API response in sessionStorage with 15-min TTL;
  serve stale data immediately on re-open, skip fetch if fresh
- VirtualGrid: accept columns prop for accurate fallback height
  (no more ~33% CLS overestimate on wide screens)

Accessibility:
- Wear-frequency dots: add role="img" + aria-label ("Worn N days ago")
- Modal: add keyboard focus trap — Tab/Shift-Tab cycles inside modal,
  first focusable element receives focus after 50ms paint delay

UX:
- inputMode="decimal" on price and case-size inputs (numeric keyboard on mobile)
- inputMode="numeric" on all lug-width inputs
- autoCorrect="off" + autoCapitalize="words" + spellCheck=false on
  watch name, garment name, and secondary garment name inputs
- Clear wardrobe button now uses .btn-danger utility class for
  consistent destructive-action styling

PWA / offline:
- SW precache list extended with core app JS modules (dev-mode paths;
  best-effort, 404s handled gracefully via Promise.allSettled)
- SW fetch handler now caches any same-origin .js/.css file
  (not just /assets/ prefix), covering both Vite-prod hashed assets
  and dev-mode flat files — improves offline-on-first-install reliability

Bump version to 68.7.0

https://claude.ai/code/session_019pS9X1R8PVd74VCEqX1VYs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix list wrapped-line indentation by using tab stop alignment

In Word, a tab character separates the bullet glyph from the text, with a
tab stop at the leftIndent position. Without this, the text after the bullet
starts at ~bullet_width from firstLineHeadIndent, while wrapped lines start
at headIndent — making subsequent lines appear over-indented.

- resolveListPrefix: suffix changed from " " to "\t" for all list formats
- buildCTParagraphStyle: adds a CTTextTab at leftIndentPt when firstLineIndentPt < 0,
  causing the tab to jump text to the same position as headIndent (leftIndentPt),
  aligning first-line text with all subsequent wrapped lines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix post-deploy audit: grep -c outputs count even on no match

When grep -c finds 0 matches, it outputs "0" AND exits with code 1.
The || echo "0" then appends a second "0", creating an invalid
GITHUB_OUTPUT entry. Fix: use || true to suppress exit code, and
${HTML_COUNT:-0} for default.

commit.message:
Use widget_id directly in wire protocol instead of broken _resolve_id

Generated(text, len) widget IDs now write their text directly as the
wire protocol string ID. This fixes the bug where _resolve_id mapped
all Generated IDs to -1, making SetClassName/SetTextContent/etc
impossible on non-root elements.

commit.message:
Fix auth: hardcode production redirectTo, handle both ?code and #access_token

- app/paywall/page.tsx line 57-59: redirectTo now falls back to hardcoded
  production URL instead of window.location.origin, preventing localhost links
- Added console.log('[AUTH] Using redirectTo: ...') to confirm value at runtime
- app/auth/callback/page.tsx: handle both PKCE (?code=) and implicit
  (#access_token=) flows, log every auth step, increase timeout to 10s,
  guard setSession/exchangeCodeForSession with explicit error paths

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: drain serial buffer fully on each read cycle

Single serial.read() returns on first byte, producing partial frames.
Now drains all available bytes using in_waiting after the initial read.
Also discards garbage bytes before the first SLIP frame start marker.

commit.message:
Add authoritative surface header to invoke API

The HTTP invoke endpoint now accepts an optional "surface" field in the
request payload. When present, it prepends a --- CURRENT TURN --- header
to the prompt with the surface and caller, ensuring agents always know
which surface the current turn came from — even when session history
has messages from other surfaces.

- http.clj: wrap-surface-header + accept surface field in handle-invoke
- ngircd_bridge.py: send surface "irc (#futon)" in invoke payload
- claude-repl.el: send surface "emacs-repl" in invoke payload, remove
  inline surface-contract function (server handles it now)
- claude-repl.el: improved auto-register with GET /agents fallback,
  init ordering fix (register before workspace derivation)

commit.message:
Add player dash/dodge ability

Adds a dash mechanic (Space key) that lets the player burst forward
in their current movement direction for quick dodging.

- Space triggers dash in movement direction (or forward if stationary)
- 800 units/sec burst speed for 200ms (~3 tiles of distance)
- 2-second cooldown between dashes
- Brief invulnerability frames (100ms) for dodging through projectiles
- Wall collision stops dash early (no clipping)
- Whoosh sound effect on dash
- Cooldown arc indicator below crosshair
- Replaces minimal jump mechanic with more useful dash

Fixes #113

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

checkpoint: UTC→Pacific timezone fix for sent_date documented

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix negative energy dashboard statistics from zero-value placeholder rows

Two bugs in history_import.py caused all lifetime-total statistics to
be written with sum=0 for the current month, producing large negative
changes in the HA energy dashboard:

1. valid_rows filter included value=0 rows. When a new billing year
   starts, the API returns the current open month as a placeholder with
   value=0. If that is the only row returned (old period absent from
   DatesSet), running_total=0 and sum=0 gets upserted into Recorder,
   zeroing out the current month's statistics.

2. Cap condition lacked a current_lifetime > 0 guard. A negative API
   aggregate for the new billing period yields a negative
   lifetime_total_value in coordinator.data. Since a non-zero negative
   is truthy, current_lifetime would be negative, triggering the cap
   and setting the last row to max(0.0, negative) = 0.

Fix: skip rows with value <= 0 in valid_rows; add current_lifetime > 0
guard to the cap condition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add spell check GitHub Action and fix misspellings

Add cspell-based spell check workflow with a project dictionary
of technical terms. Fix 7 misspellings found:
- categoiies → categories
- certin → certain
- provenence → provenance
- reqquest → request
- responsiblities → responsibilities
- restictions → restrictions
- environmentonly → environment only
